Feature Selection - Experiment 3 Sub-1 Run 1

Generated 2026-04-10 07:50  |  Based on tuned RF models (Exp3-S1 run 1)
Experiment 3 Sub-1 - Feature Selection: Feature importance computed on the Exp3 Sub-1 subsets (Exp2 Sub-2 baseline + ADD-tier candidate features). RF permutation importance (n_repeats=20) is the primary ranking signal, supported by mutual information and Pearson/Spearman correlations.

Goal: Identify which ADD-tier features genuinely contribute signal vs. which ones add noise and restrict rows. Core + Useful features (norm perm imp ≥ 0.03) will be retained in the feature-selected Exp3-S1 subsets.

Experiment 3 Sub-1 - Grab Effluent

Feature set: 24 features (ADD-tier + Exp2 Sub-2 baseline)  |  Thresholds: Core ✓ norm perm imp ≥ 0.08  |  Useful ≥ 0.03  |  Weak ✗ < 0.03.
Models used are the tuned RF estimators from Exp3 Sub-1 (run 1). Temporal features 🕐 (month, day_of_week, year) are flagged separately.

Feature × Target Importance Heatmap

Normalised permutation importance (sums to 1.0 per target column). Computed on training data (2021-2024).

Per-Target Recommendations & Detail

Core + Useful features (norm perm imp ≥ 0.03) will be kept in the feature-selected Exp3-S1 subsets. Click to expand.
